name: Stan Kenton
description: American pianist, composer, arranger and band leader (1911-1979)
abstract: Stanley Newcomb Kenton (December 15, 1911 – August 25, 1979) was an American popular music and jazz artist. As a pianist, composer, arranger and band leader, he led an innovative and influential jazz orchestra for almost four decades.

about_quotes.speaker: Ignacio Berroa
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] When I was in college a certain group of us, we would have dreams about getting a call to play with Buddy Rich or Stan Kenton.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ignacio Berroa interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 22, 2021 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Holman Bill
about_quotes.evidence: So I went as the tenor player. ... While I was on the band, he hired a trombone player... I guess that‟s why he accepted me on tenor.
about_quotes.doc_title: Holman Bill — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Curtis Fuller
about_quotes.evidence: I had heard the bands of Woody Herman... I found out his name... His name was Skip Layton, he played with the Stan Kenton Orchestra.
about_quotes.doc_title: Curtis Fuller — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Doc Cheatham
about_quotes.evidence: And what they hit on was Stan Kenton’s arrangement. And I never heard anything so bad in all my life.
about_quotes.doc_title: Doc Cheatham interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, New York City, New York, July 28, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Hal Espinosa
about_quotes.evidence: I didn’t know him, I knew of him in high school looking at the back of Stan Kenton albums.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hal Espinosa interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, March 20, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Candido Camero
about_quotes.evidence: I was fortunate enough to work and also recorded with ... Stan Kenton
about_quotes.doc_title: Candido Camero — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
